skill.name: documentation-templates
skill.description: Provides practical templates and structure guidance for READMEs, API documentation, code comments, changelogs, ADRs, and AI-friendly docs.
skill.summary: Create consistent project documentation with ready-to-adapt templates.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown fences and example text. The skill contains only documentation templates and requests read-only tools, with no executable scripts, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at eleven locations. These locations are Markdown code fences around README, API, changelog, ADR, and llms.txt templates, not executable Ruby or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside fenced Markdown examples. The skill file does not define scripts, shell commands, Ruby code, or tool instructions that execute these blocks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in ordinary prose, table headings, and comment templates. No hashing, encryption, signing, or password storage logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation placeholders and headings. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic code or unsafe algorithm use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matches Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance patterns in API and documentation examples. The cited lines describe an example endpoint, an ID parameter, an ADR prompt, and Mermaid diagrams.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is static instructional text. It does not run host discovery commands or ask the model to inspect system identity, network state, or environment details.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time deciding how to structure project docs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear templates for common developer documentation.
actual_capabilities: Provides a priority-ordered README structure with standard sections.
Defines a per-endpoint API documentation template.
Gives JSDoc and TSDoc comment guidance for functions.
Includes a Keep a Changelog style changelog template.
Includes an Architecture Decision Record template.
Adds guidance for AI-friendly documentation and llms.txt files.
limitations: Does not generate documentation from source code by itself.
Does not validate whether examples match a live API.
Does not include project-specific branding or style rules.
Does not provide executable documentation tooling.

use_cases.title: Standardize a New Repository
use_cases.description: Create a clear README, changelog, and documentation outline before a project grows.

use_cases.title: Document API Endpoints
use_cases.description: Use the endpoint template to make parameters, responses, and examples consistent.

use_cases.title: Prepare Docs for AI Agents
use_cases.description: Add llms.txt and structured sections so AI tools can understand the project faster.

prompt_templates.title: Create a README Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create a README outline for my project. Include quick start, features, configuration, documentation links, contributing, and license sections.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current README using the documentation-templates skill. Identify missing sections, unclear headings, and places where examples should be added.

prompt_templates.title: Write API Endpoint Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the API documentation template to document these endpoints. Include purpose, parameters, response cases, and a short request and response example for each endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Design an AI-Friendly Documentation Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the README, ADR, changelog, and llms.txt templates to design a complete documentation structure for this repository. Explain which files should exist and what each should contain.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a README structure for a small web service.
output_examples.output: A sectioned README plan with a title, quick start, features, configuration table, documentation links, contributing notes, and license section.

output_examples.input: An API team needs consistent endpoint documentation.
output_examples.output: A repeatable endpoint format covering route purpose, parameters, response outcomes, and concise examples.

output_examples.input: A project wants better documentation for AI coding tools.
output_examples.output: A documentation map that includes core files, key concepts, structured headings, and an llms.txt outline.

best_practices: Start with the README structure, then add deeper files only when needed.
Keep examples close to the sections they explain.
Update changelogs and ADRs during development, not after release.
anti_patterns: Do not copy every template section when it does not apply.
Do not document obvious code behavior instead of important design reasons.
Do not publish API docs without checking current routes and responses.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete README?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to produce a structured README from project details.

faq.question: Does it inspect source code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides templates and guidance. The assistant still needs project context or readable files.

faq.question: Is it only for open source projects?
faq.answer: No. The templates work for private projects, internal tools, libraries, and services.

faq.question: Does it include API documentation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a per-endpoint format for purpose, parameters, responses, and examples.

faq.question: What is the AI-friendly documentation section for?
faq.answer: It helps structure project knowledge so AI agents and RAG systems can retrieve context more reliably.

faq.question: Can teams customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly treats templates as starting points that should be adapted to project needs.
